title: Water Tiles & Functional Bridges

tasks.title: Add Water to palette and state system
tasks.description: Add 'water' to SCENERY_TYPES and SCENERY_EMOJI. Add a new palette entry in the sidebar under Scenery with a 💧 Water item. Water tiles should be treated as scenery for placement purposes.
changes: Add 'water' to SCENERY_TYPES array
Add water: '💧' to SCENERY_EMOJI (or use 🌊)
Add palette-piece div for water in sidebar HTML under Scenery section

tasks.title: Water tile CSS animation (wave ripples)
tasks.description: Create a CSS keyframe animation for water tiles. Use a blue-gradient background on water cells with animated wave ripple effect. Water cells should look visually distinct from grass. In night mode, water should be darker blue with subtle moonlight reflection.
changes: Add .cell.water-cell CSS class with blue gradient background
Add @keyframes water-wave CSS animation for subtle ripple motion
Add night mode variant: body.night-mode .cell.water-cell with darker water + moonlight shimmer
Modify renderCell() to apply 'water-cell' class when cell contains water type
Water emoji should be replaced with a proper CSS-rendered water effect (not just emoji)

tasks.title: Prevent track placement on water
tasks.description: Track pieces (including trains and cars) cannot be placed on water tiles. Show a toast message when user tries. Scenery can replace water.
changes: In onPointerUp (drag drop handler), check if target cell has water before placing track/train
Show toast '🌊 Tracks can't go on water!' when user tries to place track on water
Allow scenery to replace water (one scenery replaces another)
Allow water to replace other scenery

tasks.title: Bridge visual enhancement near water
tasks.description: When a bridge track piece is adjacent to water tiles, add a visual indicator that it spans over water. Use z-index and subtle shadow/highlight to make bridge look like it goes over the water. This is purely cosmetic enhancement.
changes: In renderCell() for bridge type, check adjacent cells for water
If water is adjacent, add a CSS class 'bridge-over-water' with enhanced visuals
Bridge-over-water gets subtle shadow underneath and slightly elevated appearance

tasks.title: Animated duck on water tiles
tasks.description: Water tiles display a small decorative duck that slowly drifts/bobs on the surface. Each water tile has a chance (~40%) of showing a duck. Ducks animate with a gentle CSS drift animation.
changes: Add @keyframes duck-drift CSS animation (gentle side-to-side + bob)
In renderCell() for water type, randomly add a small duck element (🦆) with animation
Ducks should have randomized animation delay so they don't all move in sync
Duck size should be small (~35% of cell size) so it doesn't overwhelm

tasks.title: Random generator river feature
tasks.description: The random track generator should sometimes (~40% chance) include a 'river' - a strip of water tiles that cuts through the layout. The river should avoid overlapping with track pieces.
changes: Add generateRiver() function that creates a vertical or horizontal water strip
Call generateRiver() from addRandomScenery() with ~40% probability
River should be 1 cell wide, spanning 3-6 cells in length
River avoids cells already occupied by track pieces
Add water tiles to the grid state

tasks.title: Night mode water styling
tasks.description: In night mode, water tiles should show darker blue with a subtle moonlight reflection shimmer effect.
changes: Add body.night-mode .cell.water-cell CSS with darker blue gradient
Add subtle shimmer/reflection keyframe that activates only in night mode
Ducks should still be visible in night mode (slightly adjusted opacity)

acceptance_criteria: AC1: Water tile appears in palette under Scenery section
AC2: Water tiles show animated wave ripples when placed on grid
AC3: Cannot place track/train/car directly on water cells — toast message shown
AC4: Bridge pieces adjacent to water get visual enhancement
AC5: Some water tiles display animated drifting ducks
AC6: Random generator occasionally creates river strips of water
AC7: Water tiles look correct in both day and night modes
AC8: All existing features still work (trains, cars, switches, save/load, etc.)
AC9: Water tiles can be removed (right-click/long-press) like other scenery
AC10: Save/load correctly preserves water tiles
testing_focus: Place water then try to place track on it — verify rejection toast
Place bridge adjacent to water — verify visual enhancement
Generate random track multiple times — verify rivers appear sometimes
Toggle night mode with water tiles present
Save layout with water tiles, reload — verify persistence
Verify no regressions: trains animate, switches work, cars attach, crash effects fire
